description: Re-anchor the active harness from the prior Voice Bridge task to the new AICodeWith provider-integration goal without losing prior progress history.
steps: Record in `PROGRESS.md` that the user explicitly redirected the repository to AICodeWith provider integration.
Replace `PLAN.json` with a plan that matches the new goal instead of the completed-or-stale Voice Bridge scope.
Verify the repo baseline note still records the pre-existing unrelated `tests/test_coding_mode.py` import failure separately from this task.

description: CLI startup uses the AICodeWith custom multi-route provider so `providers.aicodewith` can serve GPT/Codex, Claude, and Gemini traffic from a single API key.
steps: Update `nanobot/cli/commands.py` so `_make_provider()` instantiates `CustomProvider` when the resolved provider is `aicodewith`.
Keep the existing `providers.aicodewith` config schema and default API base behavior unchanged.
Verify the CLI route no longer forces `aicodewith` through the Anthropic provider class.

description: SDK/runtime startup uses the same AICodeWith custom provider path as the CLI so `Nanobot.from_config()` behaves consistently.
steps: Update `nanobot/nanobot.py` so `_make_provider()` instantiates `CustomProvider` for `aicodewith`.
Keep existing validation semantics for missing API keys intact.
Verify SDK startup no longer binds `aicodewith` to the Anthropic provider class.

description: Runtime model switching and provider factory helpers resolve `aicodewith` through the custom provider implementation instead of the wrong backend.
steps: Update `nanobot/providers/factory.py` so `create_provider()` returns `CustomProvider` for `provider_name == "aicodewith"`.
Reuse the resolved provider config and API base rather than hard-coding a second config path.
Verify model-switching code can activate `aicodewith` without raising a backend mismatch.

description: AICodeWith model-name normalization strips the provider prefix correctly so explicit `aicodewith/<model>` selections reach the upstream route with the right model id.
steps: Update `nanobot/providers/custom_provider.py` normalization helpers so they remove a leading `aicodewith/` prefix before the OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini route-specific normalization runs.
Preserve existing handling for `anthropic/...`, `openai/...`, `chatgpt/...`, `gemini/...`, and `google/...` prefixes.
Verify explicit `aicodewith/gpt-5.4`, `aicodewith/claude-*`, and `aicodewith/gemini-*` examples normalize as intended.

description: Provider-routing unit coverage proves the CLI, SDK, and factory all instantiate the AICodeWith custom provider instead of the wrong backend.
steps: Add or update tests in the CLI/provider/facade suites for `provider == aicodewith` instantiation.
Keep the assertions focused on provider class selection and default model/API-base propagation.
Verify the new tests fail before the routing fix and pass after it.

description: Custom-provider unit coverage proves explicit AICodeWith model prefixes are stripped before route dispatch so GPT/Codex, Claude, and Gemini requests send valid upstream model names.
steps: Extend `tests/test_custom_provider.py` with explicit `aicodewith/...` normalization assertions.
Cover at least one OpenAI-style model, one Claude-style model, and one Gemini-style model.
Verify the helper outputs the provider-free model identifiers expected by each route.

description: README provider documentation explains how to configure AICodeWith as a single-key gateway for GPT/Codex, Claude, and Gemini models in nanobot.
steps: Add `aicodewith` to the provider summary table in `README.md`.
Document the nanobot config shape, default base URL, and example model/provider settings for AICodeWith.
Verify the docs align with the actual implementation semantics instead of the old Anthropic-only description.

description: The AICodeWith integration change is checkpointed with targeted verification and progress notes so later sessions can resume from a known-good state.
steps: Run the targeted pytest commands that cover the touched runtime and custom-provider paths.
Append the verification results and any remaining baseline caveats to `PROGRESS.md`.
Commit the checkpoint once the new feature work and targeted verification are complete.
